summary: 17th-century Dutch Golden Age painter of lively, comedic genre scenes. Known for The Feast of St. Nicholas, The Merry Family, Beware of Luxury, and Children Teaching a Cat to Dance. Also worked as brewer and innkeeper. Co-founded Guild of St. Luke in Leiden. Inspired Joan Miro's Dutch Interiors series.

works.name: The Feast of St. Nicholas
works.description: Boy in gold/yellow dress delighted by gift of doll while older brother cries over birch rod. Figure in background holds a child and gestures upward; foreground has basket of various breads. Children teach a cat to dance. Domestic gift-giving scene. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Child in gold dress receives doll while older brother cries over birch rod; domestic gift-giving on St. Nicholas's Day
examples: a child in a gold dress has received all the gifts during the title celebration
a boy is crying because the only gift he's received is a lousy birch rod
a golden haired girl carries a doll and a basket of treats while her older brother sobs on her right

clues.clue: Figure in background holds a child in his right arm and gestures upward; basket of various breads in foreground
examples: One figure in the background of this painting holds a child in his right arm and gestures upwards with his other hand, and in the foreground appears a basket of various breads

clues.clue: In the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum alongside The Merry Family
examples: is displayed in the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum

works.name: The Merry Family
works.description: Large family drinking, smoking, and playing instruments around a dinner table. Man holds fiddle and raises glass. Drunk father fiddling at the left of a rowdy table.

clues.clue: Large family drinking, smoking, playing instruments; man holds fiddle and raises glass; drunk father fiddling at left of rowdy table
examples: depicted several young and old people playing instruments around a dinner table
a man holding a fiddle and raising a glass
a drunk father fiddling at the left of a rowdy table in The Merry Family
Merry Family shows a father cheerfully raising one of these objects, implying that this is leading his children to drink

works.name: Children Teaching a Cat to Dance
works.description: Children force a cat to dance on a table while a dog barks from below. Also associated with a related scene of children teaching a cat to read. Miro's Dutch Interiors series was partly inspired by this painting.

clues.clue: Children force a cat to dance on a table as a dog barks from below
examples: In a genre painting by Jan Steen, children force one of these pets to dance on a table as a dog barks from below
Families teaching these animals to read and to dance
These animals appear in both of the Jan Steen paintings which inspired two of Miro's three Dutch Interiors
An artist who attempted to 'assassinate painting' executed parodies of works of art from this country such as Children Teaching a Cat to Dance

images.caption: Children Teaching a Cat to Dance

works.name: The Doctor's Visit
works.description: Doctor in dramatic theatrical costume visiting lovesick or pregnant young women. One version features a man hanging a herring over the head of a woman receiving quack tests for pregnancy and lovesickness. A sitting Cupid-like boy puts an arrow in his bow next to an empty red chair.

clues.clue: Multiple versions: doctor in theatrical costume visiting lovesick or pregnant women; similar poses across the series
examples: This artist painted multiple theater-inspired scenes of a doctor clothed in dramatic costume visiting lovesick or pregnant young women
That painting titled for this profession is by Jan Steen and features similar poses to his paintings of women in love

clues.clue: One version: man hangs a herring over head of woman undergoing quack tests for pregnancy and lovesickness
examples: An artist from this country painted a man hanging a herring over the head of woman who's being given quack tests for pregnancy and lovesickness in The Doctor's Visit

clues.clue: Sitting Cupid-like boy puts an arrow in his bow next to an empty red chair
examples: a sitting Cupid-like boy puts an arrow in his bow next to an empty red chair

works.name: The Dissolute Household
works.description: A woman gazing at a wine glass fails to notice her husband flirting with a maid. Related to the broader theme of moral disorder in Steen's domestic scenes.

clues.clue: A woman gazing at a wine glass fails to notice her husband flirting with a maid
examples: In Jan Steen's painting The Dissolute Household, a woman gazing at one of these objects fails to notice that her husband is flirting with a maid

works.name: Burgomaster of Delft and His Daughter
works.description: Portrait of Adolf and Catharina Croeser, a wealthy merchant of Delft and his daughter. Painted after the Delft explosion of 1654. Also referred to as depicting a Burgomaster of Delft with his daughter.

clues.clue: Depicts Adolf and Catharina Croeser, a wealthy merchant of Delft and his daughter; painted after the Delft explosion of 1654
examples: a depiction of Adolf and Catharina Croeser, a wealthy merchant of Delft and his daughter
Steen painted a Burgomaster from this Dutch city

works.name: Card Game Quarrel

works.name: The So-Called Brewery
works.description: A boy creating soap bubbles appears above the scene next to a skull, evoking the Dutch Homo Bulla (soap bubble as transience of life) motif.

clues.clue: A boy creating soap bubbles appears above the scene next to a skull; references the Dutch Homo Bulla transience-of-life motif

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Dutch Golden Age painter of lively genre scenes. Brewer and innkeeper by profession. Co-founded Guild of St. Luke in Leiden. Other works: Beware of Luxury (woman balances wine glass in man's lap), The Drawing Lesson, The Christening Feast, Diogenes Searching for an Honest Man, The Sick Woman. Miro's Dutch Interiors series inspired by him.

clues.clue: Careers as brewer and innkeeper influenced his paintings of tavern scenes
examples: This artist of The Christening Feast and The Drawing Lesson painted multiple tavern scenes inspired by his profession as an innkeeper

comprehensive_summary:
Jan Steen (c. 1626–1679) was a Dutch Golden Age painter celebrated for lively, moralizing genre scenes full of humor and disorder. He is considered an exemplar of Dutch genre painting — works depicting the daily lives of ordinary people — alongside contemporaries like Vermeer, Frans Hals, and Gerard ter Borch. He worked as a brewer (in Delft) and innkeeper, professions that fed directly into his prolific output of tavern and domestic paintings. He co-founded the Guild of St. Luke in Leiden.

The Feast of St. Nicholas is his most frequently clued work. In it, a child in a gold dress delightedly receives a doll and basket of treats while her older brother sobs over receiving only a birch rod. A figure in the background holds a child and gestures upward, and a basket of breads fills the foreground. It hangs in the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum alongside The Merry Family.

The Merry Family shows a large, rowdy family drinking, smoking, and playing instruments at a dinner table, with a drunk father holding a fiddle and raising a wine glass — implying his children will follow his bad example. Children Teaching a Cat to Dance, one of several works Steen made depicting animals humiliated by children, inspired Joan Miro's Dutch Interiors series, a project Miro described as wanting to 'assassinate painting.' The first painting in the Dutch Interiors series shows a distorted dog with one green and one yellow eye, transmuting a red hat and white collar from Steen's original into the dog's entire form.

Steen's many versions of The Doctor's Visit show a theatrical doctor in dramatic costume attending to lovesick or pregnant women; one version features a man dangling a herring over the patient's head. The Dissolute Household depicts a wife too distracted by a wine glass to notice her husband flirting with a maid. In The Drunken Couple, a cat appears to look up the dress of a passed-out woman. Beware of Luxury (also called In Luxury, Look Out!) shows a woman balancing wine in a man's lap, with his unusually positioned legs inverting the proverb 'it takes strong legs to carry wealth.'

The Drawing Lesson includes a sculpture of an ox (Saint Luke's symbol) on a shelf while a teacher instructs students using a plaster nude. The So-Called Brewery includes a boy blowing soap bubbles next to a skull — evoking the Dutch Homo Bulla motif of life's transience. Foot warmers, called the 'ladies' favorite' in the emblem book Sinnepoppen, appear in several of his paintings including The Sick Woman; in Dutch Golden Age painting, their placement relative to a woman's skirt was a pictorial code for marital status. He also painted Diogenes holding a lantern while searching for an honest man, and a Burgomaster of Delft with his daughter (Adolf and Catharina Croeser) after the Delft explosion of 1654.
